epics.title: EPIC FRAMEWORK-01: Framework/App Layer Source of Truth and Developer Ergonomics
epics.goal: Define and deliver the first post-Core framework/app-layer wave without weakening the proven Core MVP boundary.
scope: Own framework/app-layer source of truth for routing ergonomics, ctx/request helpers, binding, validation, Results, configuration, services/DI, diagnostics, and example hardening.
Coordinate FRAMEWORK-01.A through FRAMEWORK-01.F as owner-approved implementation chunks.
Keep OpenAPI/doctor integration planned through Strong Plan rather than ad hoc.
nonGoals: No Node/npm/package-manager compatibility unless explicitly scoped.
No public alpha docs or marketing claims.
No PostgreSQL or SQL Server JS bridge work.
implementationRequirements: Update docs/ADRs when behavior, architecture, public API, diagnostics, or workflow changes.
Add tests that verify documented intent, not accidental current behavior.
Use SlStr / SlBytes for borrowed views.
Use SlOwnedStr / SlOwnedBytes where ownership markers exist.
Use SlArena and arena copy helpers for scoped ownership.
Use SlStringBuilder / SlByteBuilder for bounded construction.
No implicit NUL assumptions for SlStr/SlBytes.
No raw native pointers exposed to JS.
Report default, V8-gated, package, live-provider, stress, and benchmark evidence lanes separately.
testsRequired: Task-appropriate docs/static checks, CTest, golden/snapshot, cargo, or process tests.
git diff --check.
qualityGates: Read AGENTS.md and relevant source docs before editing.
Run the canonical Windows workflow or explain unavailable gates.
Run language standards scanners when relevant.
Report default, V8-gated, package, live-provider, stress, and benchmark evidence separately.
No generated/build artifacts staged.
acceptanceCriteria: Supported behavior is documented as supported, deferred, or rejected.
Tests verify documented intent, not accidental implementation details.
The PR remains a coherent bounded context.
dependencyNotes: Builds on merged PR #430 and the Core MVP proof.
Coordinate with #302 for source-input run and #318/#355-#359 for Strong Plan.
recommendedPrGrouping: Prefer grouping with related tasks only when ownership, docs, tests, and risk are shared.

epics.title: EPIC HTTP-25: HTTP/1.1 Keep-Alive and Streaming
epics.goal: Upgrade the post-MVP HTTP transport beyond close-after-response without production-edge claims.
scope: Coordinate keep-alive, sequential request loop, idle timeout, max requests, chunked request decoding, streaming response writer, and conformance/stress evidence.
nonGoals: No Node/npm/package-manager compatibility unless explicitly scoped.
No public alpha docs or marketing claims.
No PostgreSQL or SQL Server JS bridge work.
No TLS, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, WebSockets, reverse-proxy behavior, or production HTTP claims.
No framework feature implementation beyond HTTP requirements.
